title: centaurea jacea subsp. dracunculifolia
intro: Centaurea jacea subsp. dracunculifolia, also known as knapweed, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties have been linked to several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Centaurea jacea subsp. dracunculifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it has also been used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
